qtype: Percentage decrease, cost calculation, ratio to percentage, ratio sharing
tex:
(a) A 2.5-litre tin of paint costs \$13.50.
\newline
\\
In a sale, the cost is reduced by 14\%.
\newline

(i)  Work out the sale price of this tin of paint.
\newline

 Work out the cost of buying 42.5 litres of paint at this sale price.
\newline

(b) Henri buys some paint in the ratio red paint : white paint : green paint = 2 : 8 : 5.
\newline

(i)  Find the percentage of this paint that is white.
\newline

 Henri buys a total of 22.5 litres of paint.
\newline

Find the number of litres of green paint he buys.
\newline

(c) Maria paints a rectangular wall.
\newline
\\
The length of the wall is $20.5\,\text{m}$ and the height is $2.4\,\text{m}$, both correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

One litre of paint covers an area of exactly $10\,\text{m}^2$.
\newline

Calculate the smallest number of 2.5-litre tins of paint she will need to be sure all the wall is painted.
\newline
\\
Show all your working.
\newline

content.content:
A 2.5-litre tin of paint costs \$13.50.
\newline
\\
In a sale, the cost is reduced by 14\%.
\newline

Work out the sale price of this tin of paint.
\newline

Work out the cost of buying 42.5 litres of paint at this sale price.
\newline

Henri buys some paint in the ratio red paint : white paint : green paint = 2 : 8 : 5.
\newline

Find the percentage of this paint that is white.
\newline

Henri buys a total of 22.5 litres of paint.
\newline

Find the number of litres of green paint he buys.
\newline

Maria paints a rectangular wall.
\newline
\\
The length of the wall is $20.5\,\text{m}$ and the height is $2.4\,\text{m}$, both correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

One litre of paint covers an area of exactly $10\,\text{m}^2$.
\newline

Calculate the smallest number of 2.5-litre tins of paint she will need to be sure all the wall is painted.
\newline
\\
Show all your working.
\newline

qtype: graphing exponential functions, solving equations graphically, and interpreting solutions from graph
tex:
The table shows some values for $y = 2 \times 0.5^x - 1$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-1$ & $-0.5$ & $0$ & $0.5$ & $1$ & $1.5$ & $2$ \\
\hline
$y$ & $3$ & $1.83$ & & $0.41$ & $0$ & & $-0.29$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) (i)  Complete the table.
\newline

 On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2 \times 0.5^x - 1$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 2$.
\newline

(b) By drawing a suitable straight line, solve the equation $2 \times 0.5^x + 2x - 3.5 = 0$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 2$.
\newline

(c) There are no solutions to the equation $2 \times 0.5^x - 1 = k$ where $k$ is an integer.
\newline

Complete the following statements.
\newline

The highest possible value of $k$ is 

The equation of the asymptote to the graph of $y = 2 \times 0.5^x - 1$ is

content.content:
Complete the table.
\newline

On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2 \times 0.5^x - 1$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 2$.
\newline

By drawing a suitable straight line, solve the equation $2 \times 0.5^x + 2x - 3.5 = 0$ for $-1 \leqslant x \leqslant 2$.
\newline

stem.content:
The table shows some values for $y = 2 \times 0.5^x - 1$.
\newline

qtype: simplifying expressions using index laws, prime factorization, HCF, LCM, and finding cube numbers
tex:
(a) Simplify, giving your answer as a single power of 7.
\newline

(i)  $7^5 \times 7^6$

 $7^{15} \div 7^5$

 $42 + 7$

(b) Simplify.
\newline
\[(5x^2 \times 2xy^4)^3\]

(c) \[P = 2^5 \times 3^3 \times 7 \qquad Q = 540\]

(i)  Find the highest common factor (HCF) of $P$ and $Q$.
\newline

 Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of $P$ and $Q$.
\newline

 $P \times R$ is a cube number, where $R$ is an integer.
\newline

Find the smallest possible value of $R$.
\newline

(d) Factorise the following completely.
\newline

(i)  $x^2 - 3x - 28$

 $7(a + 2b)^2 + 4a(a + 2b)$

(e) \[3^{2x-1} = \frac{1}{9^x} \times 3^{2y-x}\]

Find an expression for $y$ in terms of $x$.
\newline

content.content:
Simplify, giving your answer as a single power of 7.
\newline

Simplify.
\newline
\[(5x^2 \times 2xy^4)^3\]
\[P = 2^5 \times 3^3 \times 7 \qquad Q = 540\]

Find the highest common factor (HCF) of $P$ and $Q$.
\newline

Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of $P$ and $Q$.
\newline

$P \times R$ is a cube number, where $R$ is an integer.
\newline

Find the smallest possible value of $R$.
\newline

Factorise the following completely.
\newline

\[3^{2x-1} = \frac{1}{9^x} \times 3^{2y-x}\]

Find an expression for $y$ in terms of $x$.
\newline

tex:
(a) The mass, $m\,\text{kg}$, of each of 40 parcels in a warehouse is recorded.
\newline
\\
The table shows information about the masses of these parcels.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($m\,\text{kg}$) & $0.5 < m \leqslant 1$ & $1 < m \leqslant 2$ & $2 < m \leqslant 4$ & $4 < m \leqslant 7$ & $7 < m \leqslant 12$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 4 & 7 & 15 & 10 & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Complete the histogram to show this information.
\newline

 Calculate an estimate of the mean mass of the parcels.
\newline

 A parcel is picked at random from the 40 parcels.
\newline

Find the probability that this parcel has a mass of $2\,\text{kg}$ or less.
\newline

(b) Two parcels are picked at random without replacement from those with a mass **greater than** $2\,\text{kg}$.
\newline

Work out the probability that one of them has a mass greater than $7\,\text{kg}$ and the other has a mass of $4\,\text{kg}$ or less.
\newline

(c) A van delivers parcels from a different warehouse.
\newline
\\
The box-and-whisker plot shows information about the masses of the parcels in the van.
\newline

(i)  Find the median.
\newline

 Find the interquartile range.
\newline

 Two parcels are removed from the van at the first delivery.
\newline
\\
The masses of these parcels are $2.4\,\text{kg}$ and $5.8\,\text{kg}$.
\newline

Describe the effect that removing these parcels has on the median mass of the remaining parcels.
\newline
\\
Give a reason for your answer.
\newline

content.content:
The mass, $m\,\text{kg}$, of each of 40 parcels in a warehouse is recorded.
\newline
\\
The table shows information about the masses of these parcels.
\newline

Complete the histogram to show this information.
\newline

Calculate an estimate of the mean mass of the parcels.
\newline

A parcel is picked at random from the 40 parcels.
\newline

Find the probability that this parcel has a mass of $2\,\text{kg}$ or less.
\newline

Two parcels are picked at random without replacement from those with a mass \textbf{greater than} $2\,\text{kg}$.
\newline

Work out the probability that one of them has a mass greater than $7\,\text{kg}$ and the other has a mass of $4\,\text{kg}$ or less.
\newline

A van delivers parcels from a different warehouse.
\newline
\\
The box-and-whisker plot shows information about the masses of the parcels in the van.
\newline

Find the median.
\newline

Find the interquartile range.
\newline

Two parcels are removed from the van at the first delivery.
\newline
\\
The masses of these parcels are $2.4\,\text{kg}$ and $5.8\,\text{kg}$.
\newline

Describe the effect that removing these parcels has on the median mass of the remaining parcels.
\newline
\\
Give a reason for your answer.
\newline

qtype: factorizing and sketching cubic graphs, finding equation of tangent using differentiation
tex:
(a) (i)  The equation $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$ can be written as $y = x(x - a)^2$.
\newline

Find the value of $a$.
\newline

 On the axes, sketch the graph of $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$, indicating the values where the graph meets the axes.
\newline

(b) Find the equation of the tangent to the graph of $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$ at $x = 4$.
\newline
\\
Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

content.content:
The equation $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$ can be written as $y = x(x - a)^2$.
\newline

Find the value of $a$.
\newline

On the axes, sketch the graph of $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$, indicating the values where the graph meets the axes.
\newline

Find the equation of the tangent to the graph of $y = x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x$ at $x = 4$.
\newline
\\
Give your answer in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

qtype: finding nth term of linear, geometric and cubic sequences
tex:
The table shows four sequences $A$, $B$, $C$ and $D$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Sequence & 1st term & 2nd term & 3rd term & 4th term & 5th term & $n$th term \\
\hline
$A$ & 1 & 8 & 27 & 64 & & \\
\hline
$B$ & 5 & 11 & 17 & 23 & & \\
\hline
$C$ & 0.25 & 0.5 & 1 & 2 & 4 & \\
\hline
$D$ & 4.75 & 10.5 & 16 & 21 & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Complete the table.
\newline
